Title: PpGST1, an anthocyanin-related glutathione S-transferase gene, is essential for fruit coloration in peach

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Prunus persica; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hujingmilu; Yulu; Zhongnongjinhui; blood-fleshed peach; tt19 mutant
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration; UV protection; bioactive compound
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and is essential for peach fruit coloration through anthocyanin transport/vacuolar sequestration; does not mediate proanthocyanidin accumulation in the tt19 seed-coat assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpGST1 transcript abundance strongly correlated with anthocyanin accumulation across peach peel, flesh, and cultivars (R^2=0.98). 2. PpGST1 complemented the Arabidopsis tt19 anthocyanin-deficient phenotype in vegetative tissues but not the PA-deficient seed phenotype, indicating specificity for anthocyanin rather than PA transport. 3. Co-expression of PpGST1 with PpMYB10.1 enhanced anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco leaves and peach fruit compared with PpMYB10.1 alone. 4. VIGS silencing of PpGST1 reduced anthocyanin content and fruit pigmentation in blood-fleshed peach. 5. PpGST1 localized to the nucleus and tonoplast, consistent with a role in vacuolar sequestration.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increased during peach fruit ripening; higher in deeply colored cultivar Hujingmilu than lightly pigmented Yulu; detectable in pigmented inner flesh and correlated with anthocyanin-rich tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In peach fruit, highest in ripening pigmented peel/flesh; transgene expressed in Arabidopsis tt19 and transient assays in tobacco/peach/N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; light/UV inducibility reported by this and previous work; promoter contains light-, hormone-, and stress-responsive cis-elements
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; native PpGST1 promoters from Hujingmilu and Yulu in LUC assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite correlation; Arabidopsis tt19 complementation; transient overexpression in tobacco and peach fruit; TRV-VIGS in peach fruit; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase promoter assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not complement PA-deficient seed coat phenotype of tt19, indicating substrate/process specificity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving red fruit coloration by engineering anthocyanin transport, especially in combination with PpMYB10.1; suitable for trait enhancement in peach cultivars with weak pigmentation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding sequences were identical between Hujingmilu and Yulu, but promoter sequences differed; authors infer a carrier/sequestration role rather than demonstrated glutathione-conjugating catalysis

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Prunus persica; Nicotiana tabacum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration; UV protection; bioactive compound
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive regulator of anthocyanin accumulation and fruit coloration; activates PpGST1 and enhances pigmentation especially when co-expressed with PpGST1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among PpMYB10 paralogs, PpMYB10.1 showed the strongest expression and best correspondence with anthocyanin accumulation in peach fruit. 2. PpMYB10.1 significantly trans-activated the PpGST1 promoter, whereas PpMYB10.2 and PpMYB10.3 did not. 3. Co-expression of PpMYB10.1 with PpbHLH3 further strengthened activation of the PpGST1 promoter. 4. MBS1 nearest the ATG in the PpGST1 promoter was critical for PpMYB10.1-mediated activation. 5. PpMYB10.1 plus PpGST1 produced stronger anthocyanin pigmentation than PpMYB10.1 alone in tobacco leaves and peach fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PpGST1; anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including PpDFR and PpUFGT (reported in this paper/background context)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates the PpGST1 promoter directly; also positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal positive regulator for peach anthocyanin accumulation because it controls both biosynthetic genes and the anthocyanin transport gene PpGST1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening/development; likely light-related cues inferred from anthocyanin program, but not directly established here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functionally cooperates with PpbHLH3, consistent with MYB-bHLH regulatory complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest-expressed MYB10 paralog in fruit peel; expression pattern parallels anthocyanin accumulation and is stronger in Hujingmilu than Yulu
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient overexpression in tobacco leaves and peach fruit induced anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; transient expression conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; transient overexpression in tobacco and peach fruit; dual-luciferase assay; promoter mutagenesis of MBS1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering red peach coloration; likely most effective when combined with transport capacity such as PpGST1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpMYB10.2 and PpMYB10.3 were tested but did not activate the PpGST1 promoter in this study

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Co-activation through interaction with MYB regulator
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Prunus persica; Nicotiana tabacum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration; UV protection; bioactive compound
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates increased anthocyanin accumulation and enhances PpMYB10.1-mediated activation of PpGST1, but is not sufficient alone
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpbHLH3 expression was generally higher than PpMYB10 genes during fruit development. 2. PpbHLH3 alone did not activate the PpGST1 promoter. 3. Co-expression with PpMYB10.1 significantly increased trans-activation of the PpGST1 promoter beyond PpMYB10.1 alone. 4. In tobacco transient assays, co-expression with PpMYB10.1 enhanced anthocyanin pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PpGST1 indirectly via cooperation with PpMYB10.1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent enhancer of anthocyanin accumulation as MYB partner rather than an independent determinant
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental cues during fruit growth; not specifically dissected
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with PpMYB10.1 in an MYB-bHLH regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively high in both cultivars, increasing at early stages then decreasing later
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in tobacco and N. benthamiana used for functional assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; transient expression conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; transient overexpression; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with PpMYB10.1 and PpGST1 to reinforce anthocyanin accumulation, though alone is insufficient
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because it was experimentally manipulated in results and shown to potentiate PpMYB10.1 action on PpGST1
